message.content: Adversaries may perform Account Discovery in cloud environments to list cloud accounts used for administration, services, or user access within a cloud service provider or SaaS application. This technique involves leveraging authenticated access to execute specific commands and tools to enumerate accounts. For instance, in Office 365, the Get-MsolRoleMember PowerShell cmdlet can list account names by role, while the Azure CLI (AZ CLI) command `az ad user list` can list domain users. Similarly, AWS commands like `aws iam list-users` and `aws iam list-roles` can enumerate users and IAM roles, respectively. In Google Cloud Platform (GCP), `gcloud iam service-accounts list` and `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` can list service accounts and users. Tools like AADInternals and ROADTools are also used to enumerate Azure AD users. The potential impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive information and further exploitation of cloud resources. Mitigation strategies include implementing strong authentication mechanisms, regularly auditing account permissions, and monitoring for unusual account enumeration activities.
